compiled_state.title: Capture A0 baseline fixture and per-platform slack budgets (tests/perf/a0-baseline.json)
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Task

Capture A0 (pre-refactor) per-op baseline and commit as fixture file `tests/perf/a0-baseline.json`.

### Steps

1. Create `tests/scripts/capture-a0-baseline.sh` that:
   - Runs hyperfine against the CURRENT subprocess-based dispatcher for each op
   - Captures median latency per op
   - Writes `tests/perf/a0-baseline.json` with structure:
     ```json
     {
       "captured_at": "<ISO timestamp>",
       "platform": "<uname -s>",
       "ops": {
         "show":       { "median_s": 0.23, "sc_target_s": 0.05, "slack_budget": null },
         "list":       { "median_s": 0.24, "sc_target_s": 0.05, "slack_budget": null },
         "comment":    { "median_s": 0.25, "sc_target_s": 0.05, "slack_budget": null },
         ...
       },
       "platform_budgets": {
         "linux":   { "sc1_target_s": 0.05, "slack_s": 0 },
         "macos":   { "sc1_target_s": 0.05, "slack_s": 0.02 },
         "alpine":  { "sc1_target_s": 0.05, "slack_s": 0.05 }
       }
     }
     ```
   - Slack budgets: macOS +0.02s (git-commit intrinsic floor), Alpine +0.05s (python3 fcntl.flock startup)

2. Run the script on Linux CI reference hardware and commit the resulting `tests/perf/a0-baseline.json`

3. This fixture makes `test-ticket-perf-regression.sh` pass the file-existence guard (GREEN for that check)

## Testing Mode

GREEN — enables the perf regression test to proceed past its RED guard.

## File Impact

- tests/scripts/capture-a0-baseline.sh (CREATE)
- tests/perf/a0-baseline.json (CREATE — committed after running capture script)
- tests/perf/ directory (CREATE)

## Acceptance

- tests/perf/a0-baseline.json exists with all covered ops
- capture-a0-baseline.sh is executable and idempotent
- test-ticket-perf-regression.sh passes the baseline-exists guard
